revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:平塚八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hiratsuka Hachimangu |image = [[File:Hiratsuka-hachimangu, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Asama Town|ja|浅間町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Town|1=Asama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557469}} 1-6, {{ill|Hiratsuka|en|Hiratsuka|qid=Q502199|lt=Hiratsuka City|1=Hiratsuka}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} |location= {{Coord|35|20|2.23|N|139|20|56.85|E|scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine
Special Shrine |founded = (Legendary) 68th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} |main hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = August 15 |Rites = {{ill|Kōnomachi|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnomachi|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420919}} (May 5) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|20|2.2|N|139|20|56.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii001.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Giant Lantern and Torii Gate}}]] '''Hiratsuka Hachimangu''' ({{ill|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/平塚八幡宮|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}}, Hiratsuka Hachimangū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asama Town|ja|浅間町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Town|1=Asama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557469}}, {{ill|Hiratsuka|en|Hiratsuka|qid=Q502199|lt=Hiratsuka City|1=Hiratsuka}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. The shrine's former Rank was that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the Association of Shinto Shrines. Historically, the shrine was also known as '''Tsurumine-san Hachimangu''' (鶴峯山八幡宮), and it has been referred to as "One Shrine in the Nation Hachimangu" and "Chinchi Daijin." It is one of the five shrines that participate in the traditional festival of the former {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, the {{ill|Kōnomachi|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnomachi|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420919}} held annually on May 5 in the Kami-Soroi Mountain (Kamisoroi-yama) in {{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|qid=Q385023|lt=Kōzu Town|1=Ōiso}} Kōhu Hongo, holding the status of the Fifth Shrine (Gonomiya). However, it is not listed as a shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are the following three: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Ōjin Tennō) * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (Jingū Kōgō) * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Takenouchi no Sukune no Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 68th year of Emperor Nintoku (380 AD) upon the command of the emperor after a large earthquake struck the area. The shrine was initially dedicated to Emperor Ōjin. Ancient records from the Tenpyō-Shōhō era mention that {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzō|1=Emperor Kenzō}} dedicated offerings of food, and {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} donated over 4,000 chō (approximately 4,000 hectares) of shrine lands. Additionally, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} (592-628 AD), another significant earthquake occurred, prompting Empress Suiko to dedicate a handwritten prayer to the "Chinchi Daijin" deity and commission the construction of shrine buildings to pray for national peace. Later, {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} allocated two-thirds of the local taxes to the shrine, and {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}} donated a sacred sword named "Am no Haruhiko." According to the "Records of Tsurumine-san Hachimangu Shrine in Ōnaka County, Sagami Province" (1637), the shrine received its enshrinement from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} during the Jinki era (724-729 AD), more than 130 years before the establishment of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}. This event led to the area being known as Hachiman-shō. Furthermore, the shrine was designated as a "One Shrine in the Nation" sacred site in Sagami Province by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. According to the "New Edition of Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscripts" (1841), Emperor Shōmu also offered the {{ill|Lotus Sutra|en|Lotus Sutra|qid=Q861212|lt=Lotus Sutra|1=Lotus Sutra}} to the shrine during the Jinki era. The {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}} records that on August 9, 1192 (Kenkyū 3), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} offered prayers for the safe childbirth of {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}} at "Hachimangu Shrine," donating a sacred horse. The "New Edition of Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscripts" notes that although the shrine was not referred to as the "Fifth Shrine," it was indeed recognized as such due to its position relative to the "Fourth Shrine" in the Azuma Kagami. The "Records of Tsurumine-san Hachimangu Shrine in Ōnaka County, Sagami Province" also state that the shrine was used as a military camp during the conflict between [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hōjō Ujiyasu|en|Hōjō Ujiyasu|qid=Q943643|lt=Hōjō Ujiyasu|1=Hōjō Ujiyasu}}, resulting in the destruction of the shrine buildings and ancient records. In 1591 (Tenshō 19), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated 50 koku of red seal land. During the Keichō era (1596-1615), Ieyasu personally visited the shrine and ordered {{ill|Ina Tadatsugu|en|Ina Tadatsugu|qid=Q3387913|lt=Ina Tadatsugu|1=Ina Tadatsugu}} to rebuild the war-damaged shrine buildings. Additionally, in 1646 (Shōhō 3), the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} donated two forest areas for shrine repairs. In 1765 (Meiwa 2), monk Honjo Kanshin of Hiratsuka-juku used collected funds over 18 years to donate a bronze torii gate. The "New Edition of Sagami Province Fudoki Manuscripts" describes the late Edo period shrine scenery, noting the bronze torii gate with the inscription "Tsurumine-san." In the Meiji period, the shrine was designated as a Prefectural Shrine under the Modern Shrine Rank System, and in 1873 (Meiji 6), it was renamed "Hachiman Shrine" by official decree. In 1894 (Meiji 27), Emperor Meiji's daughters, Princess Masako and Princess Fumiko, visited the shrine, planting pine trees and releasing koi into the sacred pond. After World War II and the abolition of the Modern Shrine Rank System, the shrine became a Special Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines in 1953 (Shōwa 28). In August 1978 (Shōwa 53), the shrine's name was changed back to its old name, Hiratsuka Hachimangu. == Grounds == File:Hiratsuka-hachimangu, honden.jpg|main hall File:Hiratsuka-hachimangu, haiden-2.jpg|Worship Hall File:Hiratsuka-hachimangu, keidaitorii.jpg|Inner Torii Gate == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located within the grounds, including Hiratsuka {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}, part of the "Shonan Hiratsuka Seven Lucky Gods Pilgrimage." * Shinmei Shrine - Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Wakamiya Shrine - deity: {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * Suwa Shrine - deity: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} * Tsurumine-san Inari Shrine - deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} *: In October 2016 (Heisei 28), a divided spirit was transferred from Kawayori Inari at Yasaka Shrine in the Hachiman area of Hiratsuka City. The name "Tsurumine-san" is the old name of Hiratsuka Hachimangu. * Hiratsuka Benzaiten Shrine - deity: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} *: In March 2011 (Heisei 23), a divided spirit was transferred from Benzaiten Shrine at Komagata Shrine in the Okazaki area of Hiratsuka City. It is considered the Benzaiten of the "Shonan Hiratsuka Seven Lucky Gods." * Taishi Hall - deity: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} * Sengen Shrine - deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} *: Moved from Sengen Mountain near Sogen Elementary School. The district name "Asama Town" originates from this shrine. The current building was rebuilt in August 1848 (Kaei 1). * Dōsojin File:Hiratsuka-hachimangu, keidaisha.jpg|(From left) Shinmei Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Suwa Shrine File:Benten001.jpg|Hiratsuka Benzaiten Shrine == festivals == The annual grand festival ({{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル}}) on August 15 features the {{ill|Hamaori Festival|lt=Hamaori Festival|lt=Hamaori festival|ja|浜降祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamaori festival|qq=}} where the sacred mikoshi is carried to the beach at Ōgi no Matsu Beach (Hiratsuka Beach under the Ōgi no Matsu) and enters the sea, followed by the Hama festival. {|style="border-collapse:collapse" | style="width:2em; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="width:4em; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="width:1em" |　|| style="width:2em; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="width:4em; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" | |- | rowspan=3 style="background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|'''January'''|| style="text-align:right" |1st|| || ||{{ill|Saitansai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitansai|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546824}}|| || rowspan=4 style="text-align:right; background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''August'''|| | |- | style="text-align:right" |14th|| || ||Coming of Age ceremony
{{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} Rite||　|| style="text-align:right" |14th|| || ||Eve festival |- | style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |22nd||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Taishi Hall New Year festival||　|| style="text-align:right" |15th||　||　||{{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}}
Hamaori festival・Divine Procession |- | rowspan=3 style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''February'''|| style="text-align:right" |3rd||　||　||{{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}}||　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0; text-align:right" |16th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Wakamiya Shrine festival
Archery Rite |- | style="text-align:right" |11th||　||　||{{ill|Kigensai|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節|1=Kigensetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604982}}||　|| rowspan=4 style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''September'''|| style="text-align:right" |14-16th|| || ||Bonbori festival |- | style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |17th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|{{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}}||　|| style="text-align:right" |14th||　|| ||Lantern festival |- | rowspan=4 style="background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |''' May'''|| style="text-align:right" |3rd|| ||　||Children's festival||　|| style="text-align:right" |15th|| || ||Chinchi festival |- | style="text-align:right" |5th||　||　||{{ill|Kōnomachi|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnomachi|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420919}}||　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0; text-align:right" |16th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Longevity festival |- | style="text-align:right" |22nd||　||　||Taishi Hall May festival||　||rowspan=2 style="text-align:right; background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''October'''|| style="text-align:right" |1st|| || ||Subsidiary Suwa Shrine festival |- |style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0; text-align:right" |25th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Sūkeikai Grand festival||　|| style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0; text-align:right"|17th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Jingū festival
Shinmei Shrine festival |- | style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''June'''||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0; text-align:right" |25th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|{{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}}||　|| border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''November'''||style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|23rd||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|{{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} |- | style="background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''July'''|| style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |5-7th||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|　||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|Tanabata festival||　||rowspan=3 style="text-align:right; background-color:#C0C0C0; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0" |'''December'''|| style="text-align:right" |23rd|| || ||{{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor's Birthday|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} |- | || || ||　|| || || style="text-align:right" |25th|| || ||{{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}}・Chinka festival
Toshi no Ichi (Year-end Market) |- | || || ||　|| || ||style="text-align:right; border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|31st||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"| ||style="border-bottom:1px solid #C0C0C0"|{{ill|Joya|ja|除夜祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |} ＊The "Tsukushisai" (Tsukisai) held on the 1st and 15th of every month is open for anyone to attend. File:Reitai001.jpg|annual Grand festival File:浜祭典.jpg|Hama festival File:歩射神事.jpg|Archery Rite File:Bonbori001.jpg|Bonbori festival == Access == * 8-minute walk from {{ill|Hiratsuka Station|en|Hiratsuka Station|qid=Q372513|lt=Hiratsuka Station|1=Hiratsuka Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|qid=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} * Approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Atsugi Interchange|ja|厚木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsugi Interchange|1=Atsugi Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10911044}} on the {{ill|Tōmei Expressway|en|Tōmei Expressway|qid=Q1135694|lt=Tōmei Expressway|1=Tōmei Expressway}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite|和書|author=蘆田伊人|editor=蘆田伊人|title=大日本地誌大系17 新編相模国風土記稿第3巻|publisher=㈱雄山閣|date=1962-9}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻新編相模国風土記稿3巻之48村里部大住郡巻之7|chapter=糟屋庄 平塚新宿 八幡社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/34}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 平塚新宿 八幡社}}}} * {{Cite|和書|author=黒板勝美|editor=國史大系編修会|title=國史大系 第32巻 吾妻鑑前編|publisher=㈱吉川弘文館|date=1964-7}} * {{Cite|和書|author=黒板勝美|editor=國史大系編修会|title=國史大系 第33巻 吾妻鑑後編|publisher=㈱吉川弘文館|date=1965-2}} * 全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター 編　『全国神社名鑑　上巻』　全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター　1977th year 7月 * 神道大系編纂会編　『神道大系　神社編16　駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』　神道大系編纂会　1980th year 3月 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hiratsuka Hachimangu}} * [http://www.hachiman.org/ 平塚八幡宮] - Official Website * [http://blog.livedoor.jp/hrtk8/ 平塚八幡宮 Official Blog] * [http://www.facebook.com/pages/%E5%B9%B3%E5%A1%9A%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE/195090610548764 平塚八幡宮 Page] * {{Twitter}} * {{Instagram}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|平塚八幡宮|version=102236058|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平塚八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Hiratsuka Hachimangu}} {{wikidata link|Q11482330}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hiratsuka, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoritomo]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Shrine Hiratsuka Hachimangu]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hiratsuka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
